title: Iceberg Quantum unveils breakthrough in fault-tolerant quantum computing
why:
- Iceberg Quantum has unveiled "Pinnacle," a full fault-tolerant quantum computing architecture based on a new, highly efficient class of error-correcting quantum LDPC codes.
- Pinnacle reduces the physical qubits needed to break RSA-2048 encryption from millions to fewer than 100,000, significantly lowering the hardware overhead for fault-tolerant quantum computing.
- The new architecture is compatible with multiple physical qubit modalities and Iceberg Quantum is already working with leading quantum hardware companies including PsiQuantum, Diraq, and IonQ.
- These advancements were validated using standard hardware assumptions and established numerical simulation methods, resetting expectations around the timeline for quantum computers of cryptographic relevance.
- Iceberg Quantum has secured a $6 million seed round to expand its team and research, with plans to build out architectural IP, software, and deepen partnerships with quantum hardware companies.
